subject: DevOps
title: OVHcloud Strasbourg Fire (2021)
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on OVHcloud Strasbourg Fire (2021).

hook.text: March 2021. A fire destroyed OVHcloud's SBG2 data center. 3.6 million websites went down.

buildup.text: The fire started in a UPS battery room and spread fast. The entire building was destroyed.

discovery.text: Many customers had backups stored in the same facility. When it burned, backups burned too.

twist.text: Some customers lost data permanently. Their only copies were in the destroyed data center.

climax.text: The incident proved that geographic redundancy isn't optional. Off-site backups are essential.

punchline.text: Store backups off-site. Same building is not redundancy.

quiz.question: Why did some OVHcloud customers lose data permanently?
options: Their backups were stored in the same data center that burned
OVHcloud didn't offer any backup services
The fire was caused by a cyberattack
